description:
You are acting as “backupadmin”, a non-root backup operator who has write access
    to /home/user but NO elevated privileges.  
    A small project data set is already present under /home/user/data/ and you must
    perform the weekly archive procedure described below.  
    Every step is independently verified by an automated test-suite, so follow the
    required names, paths, permissions and output format exactly.

    1.  Create a new directory
            /home/user/backup_archives
        if it does not already exist.

    2.  Create a compressed archive (GNU tar + gzip) named
            weekly_data_backup.tar.gz
        that contains the COMPLETE content of
            /home/user/data
        (directory itself and all its subdirectories).  
        The archive must end up *inside* /home/user/backup_archives/.

    3.  File-system permissions  
        • Directory /home/user/backup_archives           →  750  
        • File   /home/user/backup_archives/weekly_data_backup.tar.gz   →  640  
        • File   /home/user/backup_archives/backup_manifest.log         →  600  

    4.  Produce a manifest file called
            /home/user/backup_archives/backup_manifest.log
        with the following exact, line-oriented format
        (the test harness is case- and delimiter-sensitive):

            <absolute_source_path>|<size_in_bytes>|<sha256_hex>
            <absolute_source_path>|<size_in_bytes>|<sha256_hex>
            …
            TOTAL_FILES=<count>|TOTAL_SIZE=<bytes_sum>

        • One line per *file* that was inside /home/user/data/  
          (directories are NOT listed).  
        • <absolute_source_path> is the original, fully qualified path of the file
          before it was placed into the archive.  
        • <size_in_bytes> is the decimal byte size as reported by stat -c%s.  
        • <sha256_hex> is the lowercase hex digest of the file’s SHA-256 checksum.  
        • The order of the individual file lines does not matter, but the single
          summary line MUST be last.  
        • Field delimiter is the pipe character “|” with no spaces.

        Example layout (the *actual* paths / sizes / hashes will differ):

            /home/user/data/raw/example.txt|42|d404559f602eab…
            /home/user/data/reports/summary.txt|15|a987235fac9d5…
            TOTAL_FILES=2|TOTAL_SIZE=57

    5.  Concurrency requirement  
        While the tar archive is being created you should, in parallel, prepare the
        manifest file.  Use background jobs, subshells or pipelines as you prefer,
        but make sure the final results (archive + manifest) conform to everything
        stated above.

    6.  Verification reminder  
        • The archive must unpack cleanly and reproduce the identical directory
          hierarchy under ./home/user/data when extracted.  
        • The manifest file must pass byte-for-byte comparison against what the
          tests generate at runtime, so double-check delimiters, counts and case.  
        • The summary line TOTAL_FILES=<…>|TOTAL_SIZE=<…> must reflect the exact
          number of *regular files* found under /home/user/data and the summed size
          of those files (not the size of the archive).

    DO NOT hard-code any expected hash values; compute them programmatically so
    they always match the real input files.

    When everything is done you should have exactly two new filesystem objects
    inside /home/user/backup_archives/:

        weekly_data_backup.tar.gz
        backup_manifest.log
truth:
Initial filesystem that exists BEFORE the agent starts:

    Directory tree:
        /home/user/data/                        (700)
        ├── raw/                               (700)
        │   ├── file_a.txt                     (600)   content: "alpha\n"
        │   └── file_b.txt                     (600)   content: "beta\n"
        └── reports/                           (700)
            └── summary.txt                    (600)   content: "summary report\n"

    Byte-sizes of the three regular files that must appear in the manifest:
        /home/user/data/raw/file_a.txt              6
        /home/user/data/raw/file_b.txt              5
        /home/user/data/reports/summary.txt        15

    After successful completion the following MUST exist:

    1. Directory  /home/user/backup_archives        (mode 750)
    2. File       /home/user/backup_archives/weekly_data_backup.tar.gz
         • mode 640
         • contains an exact archive of /home/user/data (root directory inside
           archive should be “data”, preserving both subdirectories and the three
           files above with their original relative paths).
    3. File       /home/user/backup_archives/backup_manifest.log
         • mode 600
         • text lines:
                /home/user/data/raw/file_a.txt|6|<sha256_of_alpha_newline>
                /home/user/data/raw/file_b.txt|5|<sha256_of_beta_newline>
                /home/user/data/reports/summary.txt|15|<sha256_of_summary_report_newline>
                TOTAL_FILES=3|TOTAL_SIZE=26

    The test harness will:
        • unpack weekly_data_backup.tar.gz into a temporary directory and compare
          its checksum tree against the original /home/user/data.  
        • recompute size and SHA-256 of every file, then verify backup_manifest.log
          line-for-line (ignoring only the variable hash strings).  
        • check file and directory permission bits.

    NOTE: The agent does NOT need to create /home/user/data or the files inside—
          they are already present when the session begins.
